title: Automations in the first column (Item)
excerpt: I am trying to figure out how to change the first column (item) so when a form is entered it does not just say Incoming form Answer. I would like to take another column in the board and have it mirrored into ITEM column.…

posts.author: Jordian (Jordian)
posts.cookedHtml: I am trying to figure out how to change the first column (item) so when a form is entered it does not just say Incoming form Answer. I would like to take another column in the board and have it mirrored into ITEM column. Is that possible? – this would be a mirror on a single board. Or just any automation that would allow me to set what is showing in the ITEM column to make activities easier to track in the log and to avoid confusion when going through the board.

posts.author: kaylak (Kayla K)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Jordian ,

If you go into the form → Edit Form → Customize (at the top) → monday.com settings (right side bar) → Select include name question in form

It will populate a text field on the form titled “Name” I always change the name of this to be something like “Request Summary” so it is more clear what it is.

This allows you to add a question in the form which populates the item name. I believe it can only be a a text field, so I am not sure if this will work for you.

posts.author: Jordian (Jordian)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you so much!! That worked and is exactly what I needed

posts.author: bayanglobal (Joe)
posts.cookedHtml: This is outdated, how do we do this now?

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
Jordian:

I am trying to figure out how to change the first column (item) so when a form is entered it does not just say Incoming form Answer. I would like to take another column in the board and have it mirrored into ITEM column. Is that possible? – this would be a mirror on a single board. Or just any automation that would allow me to set what is showing in the ITEM column to make activities easier to track in the log and to avoid confusion when going through the board.

Hi @bayanglobal

Once you open the Edit Form tab, you’ll see a question labeled “Name.” You can rename it to whatever you prefer. Also, make sure the question is not set to hidden.
